mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns during periods of high RSAFS (above median) are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or if the rank IC over rolling 2-year windows is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 1.0% signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return after FEDFUNDS crosses 1.0% is non-negative, or if less than 55% of such instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicate a weakening labor market and slowing housing demand, presaging an economic contraction that pushes equity prices down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE monthly increase >0.2pp and HOUST monthly decline >5%
failure_condition: If the joint trigger yields a positive mean return or if the signal does not outperform a simple single-variable threshold over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL YoY >5%), persistently elevated price growth erodes real earnings and forces central bank tightening, making equities unattractive and imparting negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the negative return signal fails to hold during high inflation regimes (CPI>5%) over at least two separate episodes, or if the average 20-day return is positive during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals that the labor market is deteriorating and the economy may be entering a recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return after UNRATE breaks above 5.0% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p<0.05) or if the frequency of positive returns exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which translates into positive corporate earnings and upward momentum for the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after positive INDPRO surprises (relative to consensus) is not statistically positive, or if the IC over short horizons is negligible (<0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and declining retail sales (RSAFS) creates a dual shock to equity valuations: higher discount rates and weaker earnings prospects, pushing S&P 500 returns lower over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS monthly increase >0.25pp and RSAFS month-over-month decline >1%
failure_condition: If the composite signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of instances, or if the rank IC is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, continued payroll growth (PAYEMS) pushes the economy toward overheating, triggering inflation and monetary tightening, which ultimately depresses equity prices. Thus, robust employment gains are a bearish signal in this regime.
failure_condition: If during identified late-cycle periods (e.g., CAPE > 30, yield curve flat) the 20-day forward return after strong PAYEMS readings is positive, or if the signal fails to differentiate between early and late cycle.
